回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	112學年度第2學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 英語字彙 (English Vocabulary)	開課班級 : 應英一	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 陳宛利	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
-experience the power of narration in short stories -familiarize students with narrative techniques and perspectives (ex:“interior/ internal monologue,”“unreliable narrator,”“omniscient or limited point of view”) -develop analytical skills on narrative structure and characterization -learn the wider contexts of short stories 


2.教學綱要
This course guides students to read superb pieces of storytelling, to experience the power of narration in short stories and how contrived narratives intrigue readers, draw them in, or even shock them within episodes of character’s revelation, self-disclosure, and epiphany. What take part in this exploration are Edgar Allan Poe’s classic suspense stories: “A Tell-Tale Heart” (1843) and “Masque of the Red Death” (1842). The psychological suspense of Poe (1809-1849) stares into the darkest abyss of human nature and have readers witness scenes of bloody horror. Students will experience the storytelling masters’ distinctive narrative techniques and perspectives, writing styles as well as the recurring motifs and character growth in their works, and most important of all, enrich the interpretations of literature in dialogue with your own experiences.

Selected quotes:
"Above all was the sense of hearing acute. I heard all things in the heaven and in the earth. I heard many things in hell. How, then, am I mad? Hearken! and observe how healthily—how calmly I can tell you the whole story. "
-- Edgar Allan Poe’s “The Tell-Tale Heart”

“The ‘Red Death’ had long devastated the country. No pestilence had ever been so fatal, or so hideous. Blood was its Avatar and its seal—the redness and the horror of blood.”
-- Edgar Allan Poe’s “Masque of the Red Death”

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Assessment: Attendance, in-class performance, quiz, group presentation (40%); Midterm Exam (30%); Final Exam (30%) 
Course Requirements: Students are expected to attend all the class sessions and attend each class with the course readings. Late arrival, coming to class empty-handed, or being a phubber in class will lead to a deduction of the final grade. Be on time and come prepared to learn and contribute. Students will be selected at random to answer questions and be graded based on how well they demonstrate understandings of the reading. No make-up quizzes for your absences. Understand and be responsible for your grade. 
To facilitate learning outside the classroom, course materials and assignments will be uploaded onto E-campus, an E-learning environment for all NQU courses. All students must register for E-campus in order to gain full access to the updated course information and submit homework online. For course-related issues or questions, contact me at tmpmlily@gmail.com * 詳盡之授課規劃將於第一週上課公告及說明，並依照實際上課狀況與教學形式適度調整，請同學至Ecampus平台，課程內輸入Email信箱，以接收即時課程資訊。
